titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Francis Willoughby Frost Papers and Photographs
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason and Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 14973314.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday September 11, 2021 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Francis Willoughby Frost papers and photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence, photographs, postcards, scrapbooks, and others papers compiled by Francis Willoughby Frost relating primarily to travel while accompanying United States Secretary of War William Howard Taft on a 1905 diplomatic mission resulting in the Taft-Katsura Agreement. Locations visited include Manila, Philippines, and Tokyo, Japan.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two series: I. Papers, 1899-1915. II. Photographs, Postcards, and Scrapbooks, 1878-1919.

bioghist.head: Francis Willoughby Frost
bioghist.p: Francis Willoughby Frost (1876-1935) of Chicago, Illinois, was the son of George Henry Frost (1838-1917) and Louisa Hunt Frost (1839-1923). He was employed by the Engineering News Publishing Company of New York.

In 1905, Frost accompanied United States Secretary of War William Howard Taft on a diplomatic mission to Asia that resulted in the Taft–Katsura Agreement. Frost left Taft's party after Beijing, China, and continued to travel for nine months before returning home to New Jersey.
Frost married Alice Birney Blackwell Frost (1881-1938) in 1907. The couple had two sons, Francis Willoughby Frost (1909-1992) and George Henry Frost (1914-1969).

bioghist.head: The Taft–Katsura Agreement
bioghist.p: The Taft–Katsura Agreement was a discussion between United States Secretary of War William Howard Taft and Japanese Prime Minister Count Katsura Tarō on 1905 July 27 regarding the position of the two nations in greater East Asian affairs (particularly regarding the status of Korea and the Philippines following Japan's victory during the Russo-Japanese War).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Francis Willoughby Frost Papers and Photographs. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, photographs, postcards, scrapbooks, and others papers compiled by Francis Willoughby Frost relating primarily to travel while accompanying United States Secretary of War William Howard Taft on a 1905 diplomatic mission resulting in the Taft-Katsura Agreement. Locations visited include Manila, Philippines, and Tokyo, Japan.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Francis Willoughby Frost Papers and Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged into seven groupings: Correspondence, 1899-1915. Menus and Programs, 1905-1910. Communications, 1905-1910. Postal Items, undated. Bills, Receipts, and Ledgers, 1905-1906. Travel Documents, 1905-1906. Other Papers, 1905-1906.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I consists of correspondence primarily from Francis Willoughby Frost to brother Edwin Hunt Frost and father George Henry Frost; menus and programs; communications and postal items; bills, receipts, and ledgers; travel documents; and other papers documenting Frost's travels while accompanying United States Secretary of War William Howard Taft to Japan and the Philippines on a 1905 diplomatic mission.

did.unittitle: Correspondence
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter from George Henry Frost to Louisa [Hunt] (wife)
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Typed letter from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost with autograph edits
Typed letter, carbon copy, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost, with envelope.
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost, with envelope.
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter from Francis Willoughby Frost to unidentified correspondent
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to unidentified correspondent
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Typed letter from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Typed letter from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Reflection on India, enclosure from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Autograph letter, signed, from Francis Willoughby Frost to George Henry Frost
Envelope from Francis Willoughby Frost to Edwin Hunt Frost
Invitation to Francis Willoughby Frost to state ball
Typed letter, signed, from William Taft to Francis Willoughby Frost declining an invitation to teach.
Menus and Programs
Program for the Taft Party's reception in Honolulu, Hawaii
Program of plays and dances at the Maple Club in Tokyo, Japan
Program of entertainments for the Taft Party in Manila, Philippines
Program for the Taft Party's reception in Cebu, Philippines
Program of exercises in honor of the Taft Party in Tacloban, Leyte
Menu for banquet given to Taft Party by the people of Albay at Legaspi
Menu for dinner given for Secretary Taft for fourth annual dinner of the Philippine Party
"From Occident to Orient," a program for the itinerary of the Taft Party
Communications
Telegraph from Francis Willoughby Frost to his mother [Linda Hunt]
Note to Francis Willoughby Frost from Kanjimull Bhugwandas & Co.
Envelope addressed to Francis Willoughby Frost, courtesy of Thomas Cook & Sons
Invitation to Francis Willoughby Frost to the wedding of Miss Mignon Critten and Mr. Swager Sherley, from the parents of the bride
Invitation to Francis Willoughby Frost to the Fourth Annual Dinner of Philippine Party
Note in Japanese
Postal Items
Stamps from Johore, [British Malaya]
Envelopes and stamps from Egypt
Bills, Receipts, and Ledgers
Hotel Bill, marked "Naruko Jinger Tien," written in Japanese
Receipt from D. Andalaft & Mohamed Aly for an old scarab ring of the 18th century, 1500 B. C., for which the buyer paid 300 pounds
Red leather-encased notepad with ledger of expenses
Ledger of expenses
Travel Documents
Itinerary of travel from beginning with Calcutta and ending in Bombay
Passes allowing Francis Willoughby Frost to visit the Senate Chamber and House of Representatives
Pass allowing Francis Willoughby Frost to visit Egyptian monuments until 1906 June 30
Information card on the ocean liner "Salazie"
Other Papers
"The Philippines Revisited," article by William H. Taft extracted from The Independent, Volume 59.
Various newspaper clippings about the Taft expedition
1906 Calendar book with representations of Beato Angelico paintings
Tourist's map of India
Buddha and Japanese [Chinese?] writing stamped on rice paper
Sketch of riverbank and bridge on two pieces of paper
Various calling cards, presumably from Frost's travels
Various stickers from hotels, presumably from Frost's travels
Photographs, Postcards, and Scrapbooks

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged into seven groupings: Photographs, 1878-1919. Photograph Album, circa 1905. Photograph Album, 1925. Postcards, circa 1905. Scrapbook, 1905. Scrapbook, 1905. Scrapbook, 1905.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II consists of photographs, photograph albums, postcards, and scrapbooks compiled by Francis Willoughby Frost. Circa 2,000 loose photographs, circa 185 postcards (blank), and three scrapbooks (disbound) document Frost's travels while accompanying United States Secretary of War William Howard Taft to Japan and the Philippines on a 1905 diplomatic mission.

did.unittitle: Photographs
Photographs of Peking and Shanghai, including views of the Summer Palace of the Manchu Emperor, views of the Temple of Heaven, views of railways, and portraits of priests
Photographs and negatives of Java, including a view of native boys carrying water, views of a pier, and various views of architecture
Photographs of the Frost Family, including portraits of Francis, Edwin, and George Frost
Negatives, presumably from Frost's travels, probably of Egypt, including views of men in turbans and camels
Photographs of Mount Fuji taken from various locations

Photographs of Greece. Photographs of Athens include a view of the Public Library, a view of a stadium, an elevated view of the city, and a view of the Acropolis. Also included is a view of the Harbor of Patras
Photographs of Mortise, including an elevated view of the city and a view of uniformed men walking down a street

Photographs of Ceylon (now Sri Lanka) by Plâté & Co. Photographs of the rubber industry include a view of an elephant clearing ground for rubber, a view of a rubber tree, and a view of a man tapping a rubber tree. Photographs of Colombo, the capital city, include a view down York Street, a panorama of the landing jetty and Colombo Harbors, a view of Catham Street, and a view of breakwater. Other photographs include views of elephants, a view of Singhalese villagers, and a view of the Railway to Newara
Photographs (numbered prints)
Photographs (unnumbered prints)
Photograph Album
Photograph album primarily of an unidentified woman, possibly Alice Birney Blackwell Frost
Photograph Album
Photograph album presented by son George Henry Frost to father Francis Willoughby Frost
Postcards
Postcards from Japan and the Philippines
Scrapbook

Photographs of Japan, including group portraits in a restaurant, a view of the entrance of a park in the Asakusa district of Tokyo, and views of the Sensō-ji Buddhist temple, also known as Asakusa Kwannon
Photographs of Tokyo, including views of the Stone Torii at the foot of Atago-Yama, a view of the Buddhist Temple of Sengakuji, and a view of the entrance to the Tombs of Forty-Seven Ronins
Photographs of Tokyo, including views of the Tombs of Forty-Seven Ronins and street scenes in the city
Photographs of Japan, including street scenes of Tokyo and scenes on the Tokaido River from Tokyo to Kyoto, accompanied by newspaper clippings about the diplomatic mission
Photographs of Japan, including scenes on the Tokaido Railway from Tokyo to Kyoto, accompanied by two postcards of Kioto and a baggage tag from the Yaami Hotel
Photographs of the Kyoto Yaami Hotel, including views of the approach to the hotel and scenes around the hotel itself
Photographs of Kyoto, including views of Kyoto from the Yaami Hotel, a scene of the Taft Party's baggage, a view of the Yasake Pagoda, and views of the Kiyomizu-dera Temple
Photographs of Kiyomizu-dera Temple, including a view of the Dragon Fountain, a scene of a woman pilgrim praying, and a view of the [Army?] Lotus Fountain
Photographs of the Higashi Honganji (Buddhist) Temple and a print illustration of the temple with Japanese writing
Program for Cherry Dance given by Municipality of Kyoto for Taft Party and photographs of an undesignated temple
Photographs of Kyoto, including views of the Chion-in Temple grounds, one entitled "running to see the 'Princess'", and a view of the Imperial Palace
Photographs of Kyoto street scenes, including a view of school children and civilians cheering the Taft Party, some waving American and Japanese flags
Photographs of Kyoto, including a view of school children cheering the Taft Party and views of the Taft Party leaving Kyoto
American flag on recto, Japanese flag on verso
Photographs of the Inland Sea and a newspaper photo of a crowd in Kobe, Japan enthusiastically receiving the Taft Party
Photographs of the Shimonoseki Straits, including views of the straits and the escort of Japanese gun boats and tugboats at Moji
Photographs of Nagasaki, including views of the entrance to Nagasaki Harbor, a view of the Manchuria coaling at Nagasaki, and scenes of Japanese fencers and wrestlers on board "Siberia"
Photograph of martial art demonstration and newspaper clippings about "The Taft Party's Reception in Japan"
Photographs of Formosa (east coast) en route from Nagasaki to Manila
A clipping and a newspaper photo about Alice Roosevelt's clothed plunge into the water and a folded map of the Phillippines
Photographs of Manila Bay, including views of breakwater, views of the battle ship "Ohio," and a view of the U.S. Transport "Logan"

Photographs of Manila Bay, including views of Russian ships, a view of the dispatch boat "Rainbow", and a view of the S. S. Manchuria along with a newspaper photo of the Taft Party visiting aboard the Manchuria
Photographs of Manila Bay, including a view of the Auda monument and a view of passengers landing at Captain of the Port's dock, and newspaper photos of the Taft Party's movements

Photographs of Manila, including a view of Secretary Taft at the Dock, a view of mounted troops waiting to escort Sec. Taft from the Dock, a view of carriages arriving for the reception of Sec. Taft at the Ayuntamiento, and a view of the court at the Army and Navy Club
Photographs of the Army and Navy Club at Manila, and a Program of entertainments for Secretary Taft from the City of Manila

Documents from the Taft Party's stay at Manila, including invitations from the Army and Navy Club, and invitation from the Manila Jockey Club, and a booklet denoting the addresses of the Taft Party during their stay
Calling cards and a letter from the office of the Governor-General enclosing a ticket to a parade
Tokens from the Manila Electric Railroad allowing rides on any line, including a token depicting the governor and a token depicting Taft, and a sentence from a newspaper clipping
Newspaper clipping entitled "Trip Around the World: Francis W. Frost Will Go with Taft Party to Philippines, Sailing Saturday from San Francisco"
Map showing the route of the "American Floating Expedition"
A photograph of Secretary Taft and an unidentified young man above the S. S. Manchuria
Letter to "E. H. Frost" about logistics of joining the Taft Expedition and the itinerary for the trip
Itinerary for Taft Expedition and list of estimated expenses
Letters regarding E. H. Frost, including one introducing Frost to a Colonel Clarence and another responding to Frost's brothers thanks
Telegraphs to and from Frost regarding his departure and imminent journey

Photographs of various places, including views of the Union Pacific Railway, a view of the United States Hotel in San Francisco, a portrait of Miss Mignon Critten, a view of baggage being loaded on the S. S. Manchuria, and a view of the Taft Party boarding the S. S. Manchuria
Booklet entitled "From Occident to Orient, Being the Itinerary of a Congressional Party Conducted to the Far East by Secretary of War Taft, 1905, as Guest of the Philippine Government
Calling cards and two booklets, entitled "The Personnel of Secretary Taft's Party" and "Passenger List Souvenir, Japan and China," the latter marked, "F. W. Frost, Room 16"
Photographs of San Francisco, including a view from a steamer, a view in the bay, and views from the Golden Gate Bridge. Photographs of the Taft Party [aboard the S. S. Manchuria]

Posed photographs of the Taft Party aboard the S. S. Manchuria, including views of the party getting ready for Burr McIntosh's picture, a portrait of the House of Representatives and their wives, a view of a camera battery taking pictures of the whole party, and a portrait of the Senators and their wives. Unbound but included is a newspaper photo of the entire party.
Portraits of various groups of the Taft Party posing with Secretary Taft, including the members of his table, officers from the Army, members from Yale University, and members from Harvard University
Photographs aboard the S. S. Manchuria, including an unidentified group portrait, a view of congressmen playing cards, and four views of men asleep in rocking chairs
Photographs aboard the S. S. Manchuria, including scenes showing Colonel Edwards, Clough Anderson, Burr McIntosh, Miss Taylor, Reyburn, and Captain Saunders of the S. S. Manchuria
Photographs aboard the S. S. Manchuria, including scenes showing Ackert and Miss A, a group portrait, and that same group taking refreshments
Photographs aboard the S. S. Manchuria, including a scenes of a group of men smoking and a photograph of a man asleep
Photographs of the Fire Drill aboard the S. S. Manchuria, including views of passengers lined up on deck, a view of the stewards lined up, the steward's bucket brigade

Photographs of scenes aboard the S. S. Manchuria, including a view of the Fire Drill, a scene of Japanese children in steerage, a view with a Korean girl, a view of a Chinese Gambler in the after deck playing Chuck-o'-Luck, and a view of a game of roulette on the after deck
A photograph of the crew of the S. S. Manchuria. The badge of the Taft Party, Honolulu. Various pamphlets about Hawaii, where to visit, the Oahu Railway, and the reception of the Taft Party

Postcards of Hawaii, of the Royal Hawaiian Coat of Arms, a Flower Market, Natives Pounding Poi in Honolulu, a hut among palm tress, Rainbow Falls in Hilo, and the Ancient Hawaiian Battle Ground of Pali

Photographs of Hawaii, including a portrait of Francis Frost wearing a Lei, scenes of the Taft Party at Nuuanu Pali, a view of the front of the Royal Hawaiian Hotel in Honolulu, and a view from the back of the same hotel
Photographs of Honolulu, including views of Waikiki beach, the Maumi Hotel, and the harbor, and the scene of the Taft Party leaving Honolulu
Photographs of the S. S. Siberia on the way from Hawaii to Japan
Newspaper photos and clippings about the Taft Party aboard the S. S. Manchuria

Items pertaining to relations between Japan and the United States, including a painting of a crossed Japanese and American flags [on rice paper], a clipping about "Jap Ovation for Americans," and a newspaper photo of Alice Roosevelt and Secretary Taft arriving at Yokohama, Japan

Newspaper clippings about the Taft Party in Japan, including a newspaper photo of Taft Party disembarking from the S. S. Manchuria and a newspaper clipping headlined "Taft Party Sees Japanese Influence Gradually Reaching Toward America"

Photographs of Yokohama, including a view of the "American Friendly Association Boat" entering Yokohama Harbor, a view of the harbor, a view of Miss Alice Roosevelt on the pier, views of rickshaws, and a view of Yokohama's Theatre Street

Items pertaining to the Taft Party's visit to Yokohama, including a photograph of a Yokohama street, a newspaper clipping about Japan's enthusiastic welcome for the Taft Party, and an itinerary for the Taft Party's time in Yokohama, marked "F. W. Frost"

Photographs of Tokyo, including a view of decorations in front of Shimbashi Station, a newspaper photo with caption "Miss Roosevelt in Tokio", a newspaper photo with the caption "Gayly decorated street-car in Tokio, indicating the elaborate arrangements made in honor of Secretary Taft and Miss Roosevelt", and a newspaper photo with the caption "Hirokogi Street"

Photographs and cards from Tokyo, including an illuminated card from the Mitsukoshi Dry Goods Store, a postcard from the Imperial Hotel in Tokyo, a photograph of the view in front of the Shimbashi Station, a photograph of Senator Warren below crossed Japanese and American flags, and a decal from the Imperial Hotel

Photographs of Tokyo, including a view of the exterior of the Imperial Hotel, a scene with Mr. Chas Clark of Hartford "Courant" and Mrs. Godschaux in rickshaws, a view of the banquet hall in Imperial Hotel, and halftone portraits of the emperor and empress of Japan

Items pertaining to the Taft Party's visit to the Imperial Palace, including a seating chart for their dinner and a newspaper photo of the Taft Party posing with Japanese officials in front of the Imperial Japanese Palace

Items pertaining to dinner with General Count Katsura, Minister for Foreign Affairs, including a printed invitation issued to Mr. Francis Frost, the dinner card of F. W. Frost, and a newspaper clipping about the dinner from the Nagasaki Press

Items pertaining to the American Minister to Japan's garden party, including an invitation to Francis Frost; a newspaper photograph portrait of the minister, Lloyd Griscom, in his private office; a newspaper photograph of the American Legation at Tokio; and a newspaper photograph of Miss Roosevelt and the Japanese Princesses at the garden party

Items pertaining to various social functions in Tokyo, including a newspaper clipping about Taft Party being "forced to appear at Mikado's court in borrowed frock coats and top hats," a newspaper photograph and clipping about Miss Roosevelt dressing Japanese costume during a visit to Countess Oyama, an invitation to dinner at the Maple Club from the Tokyo Bankers and Merchants to Francis Frost, and a dinner card

Items pertaining to various social functions in Tokyo, including the dinner card of Miss Hisako Oyama, a photograph of boxes of confectionery presented to guests at the Maple Club (Bankers and Merchants) dinner, a newspaper clipping captioned "A Japanese Theatrical Performance before Miss Alice Roosevelt at Maples Club, Tokio," and a newspaper clipping entitled "Taft Party Kept Busy"

Items from social functions in Tokyo, including the menu printed on paper fan from the Bankers and Merchants Dinner and the musical Program letter-pressed on a silk fan for performances by the Japanese Imperial Band at General Geraouchi's Luncheon at Arsenal Gardens

Photographs of wrestlers at General Taraouchi's Luncheon at the Arsenal Gardens, including a photograph with the Taft Party in the background, a newspaper photograph of the champion wrestlers, and a clipping detailing the event

Photographs of Tokyo, including views of the Mortuary Temple and Tombs of the Tokugawa Shoguns at Uyeno Park; a view of the Temple of Zojoji in Shiba Park; a view of a zoo in Uyeno Park; a view of Chapin, Woods, and Shinosaki in a Japanese restaurant near Uyeno Park; and a view of O Jan surrounded by food trays
Album binding (empty)
Scrapbook

Visitor cards for Frost from clubs in Manila, Philippines, including the Manila Lodge, No. 761, of the Benevolent and Protective Order of Elks, the Columbia Club, and the United States Club, as well as an invitation to a banquet held by the Chambers of Commerce and Property Owners Association of Manila

Photographs of the Philippines. View of Manila include views of streets, the Santo Niño de Tondo Church, and the Army and Navy Club. Views in Cebu include Frost in front of the Basílica Menor del Santo Niño, as well as a view of a monument to Ferdinand Magellan at Punta Engaño, Mactan Island

A Program for banquet held by the Chambers of Commerce and Property Owners Association of Manila, detailing the menu, toasts, and guest-lists, accompanied by a paper with the order of arrangement for the dinner

Invitations from educational institutes to the Taft Party, including an invitation from the president of the Liceo de Manila to confer the title of "Honorary President" upon Taft, a program for the events of that reception, and a letter from the superintendent of the Bureau of Education to Francis Frost inviting him to visit the Philippine Normal School and the Philippine School of Arts and Trades

Invitations to various social events around Manila, including an invitation from the Rear Admiral Train for an boating expedition to Cavite, an invitation from Major and Mrs. Williams for an unspecified event at the Army and Navy Club, a card from the Manila Club to Francis Frost nominating him as an Honorary Member of the club, and an invitation from the Manila Club for an unspecified event

Invitations to various social events around Manila, including an invitation from the Filipino ladies of the City of Manila to "Mr. Frost, Correspondent, 'Engineer News'" for the reception given to Miss Alice Roosevelt and the Ladies of the Secretary of War's party, an invitation from the University Club to Frost for a reception, and a visitor card for Frost from the University Club

Invitation from the Saint Paul's Hospital for a lecture by the Honorable W. Bourke Cockran, along with a ticket for said lecture, and an invitation from the Reverend P. Rector of the Universidad Pontifica to Francis Frost for a reception in honor of Secretary Taft
Invitations to various social events around Manila, from the Germinal Cigar Factory, the Bureau of Government Laboratories, and Mr. Benito Legarda to receptions

Items pertaining to the Taft Party's visit to the Philippines. Documents of Francis Willoughby Frost include his visa, embossed with the seal of the Government of the Philippine Islands and detailing his physical description to the nose, issued 1905 August 29, and his Certificate of Registration for having paid a cedula personal tax of one peso. Also included is a newspaper photograph of a conference on the sugar industry in the Philippines

Photographs of the Philippines, including a panorama of the moat around the Old City Wall and a view of the Atlantic, Gulf, and Pacific Company's quarry in Manila Bay a few minutes before the blast was set off
Photographs of Atlantic, Gulf, and Pacific Company activity, including a view of their quarry in Manila Bay 10 minutes after the blast and two panoramas of the new breakwater built by the company
Photographs of Atlantic, Gulf, and Pacific Company activity, including a view of Derrick laying large stones in breakwater and a panorama of dredging and filling work in Manila Harbor
Photographs of Manila, including views of the Pasig River, a view of the Park in front of the Ayuntamiento Manila, views of the Malacañan Palace Grounds, and a view of Manila Street

Photographs of Manila. Photographs of escolta include views of them on the street, a view of them near the Bridge of Spain, a view of them distributing free cigarettes. Other photographs include a view of street work and a view of a plaza

Photographs of Manila. Photographs of street scenes include a view of a woman and children in a residential area and view of carts pulled by carabaos (water buffaloes). Also included is a view of Malacan Drive lined by wind-swept palms after a typhoon

Photographs of Manila. Photographs of street scenes include views of carts pulled by carabaos (water buffaloes) and a view of a man walking with a large load atop his head. Photographs of a cock fight include views from outside and inside the gates

Photographs of Manila, including a view of a Filipino Band, a view of a Filipino woman "Taylor" with a little boy, a view of a carabao (water buffalo) swimming, a view of a small river, and views of cascos on the Pasig River from the Bridge of Spain

Photographs of Manila churches, including views of the exterior and interior of an unidentified church, views of the Church of Saint Agustin at Intramuros, and a view of a lion statue before the Church of Saint Agustin

Photographs of Manila, including a view of a barred metal door, a view of an open square, a view of the South Gate of the Old City Wall and the Augustine Monastery, a view of the Calle Real and the Augustine Monastary, and a solitary view of the South Gate of the Old City Wall
Photographs of Manila, including a view of Augustine Monastary, a view of the North Gate in the Old City Wall, and views of street scenes with horse-drawn carts under deciduous trees

Photographs of a parade given for the Taft Party in Manila. Photographs of the Taft party include a view of Secretary Taft and Vice-Governor of the Philippines Luke E. Wright and a view of Taft, Wright, General Corbin, Alice Roosevelt, and other reviewing a parade in their honor. Photographs of the parade include a view of a procession of Manila policemen, a view of native students of the Geodetic Survey, and a view of native students drafting

Photographs of a parade given for the Taft Party in Manila, including a view of Filipino schoolboys on parade, a view of a float with Filipino girls, two views of Igarottes with views and arrows on the largest piece of hard wood ever cut in the Philippines, a view of the Manila "White Wings," and a panoramic view of the parade passing the Taft Party's reviewing stand
Photographs of Manila, including three views of Filipino Scouts at the parade in honor of the Taft Party and three views of the residence of Mr. William MacLeod and Captain Palmer on Nagtaham Road
Photographs of the MacLeods' house in more rural Manila, including views of the second floor, hall, and living room and views of the yard and balcony as seen from the room occupied by Francis Frost

Photographs of the Philippines. Photographs of rural Manila include views of thatched roofed residences next door to the MacLeods'. Photographs taken near Imus, Cavite, include a view of an old Spanish bridge and a church wrecked during the Filipino war

Photographs of the Taft Party in the Philippines. Photographs of the Manila-Dagupan Railway include a view of Alice Roosevelt and Miss Wright-Anderson [presumably the daughter of Vice-Governor Luke E. Wright] in the rear car on an excursion to San Fernando and a view of a rice field from the railway. Photographs of Malolos, capital of Aguinaldo, include a view of its train station and a panorama of the people assembled to greet Secretary Taft. On the back of the panorama is a portrait of Alice Roosevelt

Photographs of the Philippines. Photograph of Malolos include views of a demonstration against internal revenue and a view of Secretary Taft and Alice Roosevelt descending steps under a "Welcome" sign. Photographs of San Fernando include views of a demonstration with Filipino Constabulary and officers

Photographs of San Fernando, including a view of Filipino Ladies' Dresses, a scene in the Bamboo and Nipa Palm Auditorium and Banquet Hall with prominent American delegates, views of Governor Arnado and Filipino Girls, and a view of Colonel Hill running after a train

Peticiones de la Provincia de Bulacan I. F. a la Comitiva Taft [Petitions of the Bulacan Province to the Taft Party] addressed to American Congress and Memorandum about baggage arrangements for travel on the S. S. Manchuria

Items pertaining to the Taft Party's travels to the southern islands. Documents include a circular to Mr. Frost denoting his room and table, explaining protocol aboard the Logan, and advising that because alcohol in the southern islands is inferior, any alcohol should bought in Manila, and a baggage tag "No. 263 to San Francisco Cal." Photographs include a view of Taft, Forbes, Curtis, and Newlands, a view of the harbor at Iloilo, and a view of the Taft Party preparing to go ashore at Iloilo

Photographs of Iloilo, including a view of the Logan at Iloilo, a view of General Corbin, Senator Scott, and Governor Wright descending the stairs from the upper deck of the Logan at Iloilo, a view of Secretary Taft and Alice Roosevelt on the landing stage, a view of the entrance to the river, and a view of a government boat
Photographs of Iloilo, including views of the landing and a view of a Filipino Band at the Launch
Documents pertaining to the Taft Party's visit to Iloilo, including a Program of the Reception in Iloilo of Honorable William H. Taft and Party and a seating chart for a banquet

Items pertaining to the Taft Party's visit to Iloilo. Documents include an embossed menu for a banquet given for the Taft Party and an invitation to Francis Frost for said banquet. Photographs include a view of a crowd in front of a provincial building before the parade at Iloilo, a view of U.S. troops passing Taft, and a view of a native band of violins and flutes in the parade

Photographs of Iloilo, including views of school children on parade, a view of Filipino guests at a reception, views of street scenes in a more poor and more wealthy area, and a view of the landing at the harbor

Photographs of Iloilo, including street scenes with carts drawn by carabaos (water buffaloes), a view of "Uncle Tom" Cary on a carabao in a Sugar Mill near Iloilo, and scenes of Filipinos standing by thatched roof buildings near Iloilo

Photographs of the Philippines. Photographs taken near Iloilo include a view of Filipino people in the shade, a view of elevated buildings (as a precaution against flooding?), and a view of a cart and child in the sun. Photographs in Jaro include a view of a house where Jusi cloth is made, a view of the Jaro Cathedral Belfry. A photograph of Molo is view of Molo Cathedral
Items pertaining to the Philippines. An item from Jaro is a Jusi cloth handkerchief edged in yellow. Photographs of Molo are two views of the Molo Cathedral

Photographs of the Taft Party's raft ride at Bacolod, including a view of the launch, a view of a native boat, a view of the raft specially constructed for the Party of bamboo, sugarcane, and maybe thatch, and views of the Taft Party aboard the raft
Photographs of the Taft Party's raft ride at Bacolod, including a view of Taft on the raft, a view of string musicians in a native dugout, and multiple views of the raft and dugout together

Photographs of the Taft Party's raft ride at Bacolod, including a view of "coolies" pulling the raft to pier, a view of the raft (with prominent American flags) and Prado(?), a view of Secretary Taft, governor Wright, and Senator Newlands going ashore, and two more views of Taft moving between the raft and pier

Photographs of Bacolod, including a panoramic view of the raft with "coolies pulling," various members of the Taft Party, and musicians in the dugout; a view of the bamboo pier; a view of plants and elevated houses in Bacolod; and a view of a native girl carrying a chicken.

Photographs of Bacolod, including a view of a carabao-pulled cart "Caranietto"; a view of people assembled near a palm tree; a view of a Filipino grinding corn; a view of carabao and buggy crossing on a ferry; a view of guests arriving for banquet, prominently showing American contractor Wood with his Mestizo wife; and a view outside the banquet hall

Items retaining to the Taft Party's visit to Bacolod, including a view of Mrs. Shirley, Miss Critten, and Swager Sherley; a view of a boat leaving a beach; and a metallically embossed menu for a banquet in honor of the Taft Party

Photographs of Bacolod, including views of street scenes; a view of a coconut grove, a view of a carabao cart,; and a view of a "native shack" with four Filipinos, notably a young boy carrying a crudely made (probably toy) bow and stick that serves as an arrow

Documents pertaining to Negros Occidental, including a petition to the Taft Party authorized by people of the Negros Occidental province, a program of activities for the Taft Party for 1905 August 16, and "Programa de la Manifestacion"

Items pertaining to the Taft Party's visit to Zamboanga, Mindanao, including a newspaper clipping regarding the visit, a Program of entertainment, and photographic views of the ocean with various vessels

Photographs of Zamboanga, including views of boats on water, a view of a Filipino boy holding a tasseled umbrella for Datto Manda (a Moro chief), views of Filipinos waiting for the Secretary, and a view of Mrs. John T. Pershing with Datto Mande
Photographs of Zamboanga, including views of Moros Dattos going to meet Secretary Taft and views of Secretary Taft greeting Moro chiefs

Photographs of Zamboanga, including a view of Secretary Taft meeting Datto Manda; a view of the Sultan of Mindanao and staff; and three group views of Secretary Taft, Miss Roosevelt, the Sultan of Mindanao, Governor Wright, Mrs. Wright, General Corbin, and others
Photographs of Zamboanga, including views of an old Spanish fort, a view of a shrine within the fort, and a faded view of Moro boys
Album binding (empty)
Scrapbook

Photographs of the Philippines. Photographs of Sulu include a view of native boats and a festival. Informal portraits at Zamboanja include views of Secretary Taft, Miss Roosevelt, Governor General Wright, General Corbin, the Sultan of Mindanao, Datto Manda, and Mrs. John T. Pershing. Also included are a view of a native band on an insular Coast Guard boat in the Tacloban-Juanico Strait, a view of the strait, and a view of the Myin Volcano at Legaspi

Photographs of the Philippines. Photographs of Legaspi include views of Myin Volcano and a view of pier decorated with American flags. Photographs of Cebu include a view of Sorojan, a view of the Spanish Merchants' pier, a view of the interior of the Church of Black Image, and a photograph of Magellan Monument
Photographs of Philippines, probably Cebu, including views of a group of natives, a view of a native band on parade, a view of Cosco at landing, and multiple views of boats and buggies at landing

Photographs of Cebu, including a view of the Church of Second Bambino (Minor Basilica of the Holy Child), views of the Cebu Metropolitan Cathedral, a view of native traders on board an undesignated boat, a view of a public school float, a view of a civic float, and a view of native women walking down a street

Photographs of the Philippines, probably Cebu, including a view of women traders in a dugout, a view of children diving for pennies, views of the Taft Party wading ashore to Magellan Monument and back, a view of W. J. Johnson going ashore alone, a view of a school parade, and a view of a Native constabulary officer
Photographs of the Philippines, probably Cebu, including close-up views of "the Sultan" and the Sultan on a grandstand
Album binding (empty)
